stories.headline: Hyperliquid whales flip aggressively long on BTC even as funding stays deeply negative
stories.tldr: Large Hyperliquid traders have moved from net short to their most net-long BTC positioning since early March, despite persistently negative funding rates.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Whales paying to be long while retail pays to be short is a textbook squeeze setup heading into a thin-liquidity weekend.
the_details: Positioning flipped as BTC climbed from the mid-$60Ks to near $80K, per CoinDesk derivatives data published April 26.
Negative funding means shorts are paying longs — yet smart money is still adding leveraged exposure on top.
30-day BTC/DXY correlation has deepened to -0.90, the most extreme reading since 2022.

stories.headline: April crypto hacks blow past $620M as Kelp DAO drained for $292M in worst month since Bybit
stories.tldr: April 2026 is now the worst single month for crypto theft since Bybit in February 2025, with bridge failures and admin exploits dominating.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Two incidents account for 95% of losses, and Jefferies warns the streak could chill Wall Street's appetite for DeFi tokenization.
the_details: Kelp DAO's rsETH bridge was drained for ~$292M on April 18 via a LayerZero vulnerability, stranding wrapped ETH across 20 chains.
Drift Protocol on Solana lost $285M on April 1 in an attack authorities suspect was North Korea–affiliated.
DeFi has logged 47 incidents YTD in 2026, up 68% YoY versus the same window in 2025.

stories.headline: 100+ crypto firms pressure Senate Banking to mark up market structure bill this spring
stories.tldr: A coalition led by Coinbase and Ripple is pushing the Senate Banking Committee to advance the CLARITY Act before the summer recess.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Codifying the SEC/CFTC token taxonomy in statute would lock in the March 17 joint guidance and unblock institutional capital.
the_details: Letter signed by 100+ firms delivered April 23, urging markup before the summer recess clock runs out.
Builds on the March 17 SEC-CFTC joint interpretive release that superseded the 2019 'Investment Contract' framework.
Senate Banking has yet to schedule a markup; House version cleared committee earlier in 2026.

stories.headline: SEC staff: DeFi front-end operators don't need broker-dealer registration
stories.tldr: An April 13 SEC staff statement carves out 'Covered User Interface Providers' from broker-dealer registration when facilitating crypto asset security trades.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: This is the first concrete federal green light for non-custodial DeFi front-ends since the Atkins SEC took over.
the_details: Exemption applies to interfaces that don't take custody, route orders, or earn transaction-based comp.
Sidley Austin and Latham analysts call it the most consequential staff statement of the Atkins era.
Decentralized exchange aggregators and wallet UIs are the most direct beneficiaries.

stories.headline: BTC tracks for best month in a year as $5B in fresh USDT fuels rebound
stories.tldr: Bitcoin is up over 13% in April and holding above $77K, on pace for its strongest monthly performance in a year after a prolonged losing streak.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: USDT supply expansion of ~$5B this month signals fresh stablecoin liquidity is rotating directly into BTC, not just sitting idle.
the_details: Tether issued roughly $5B in new USDT in April, the largest monthly print since late 2024.
BTC's push toward $80K stalled this weekend as Strait of Hormuz risks lifted oil and tightened financial conditions.
U.S.-Iran ceasefire is set to expire in coming days — a binary macro event traders are watching closely.
